# UC-22.8.32 · SOC 2 CC6.7 — System boundary & data-transmission control: unapproved egress destinations

## Description

Evaluates outbound flows against the per-service approved-egress registry. A flow to an unapproved ASN or country is a direct CC6.7 boundary violation.

## Value

Provides the evidence SOC 2 auditors want: proof that outbound flows are governed and deviations are captured. Supports reduction of shadow SaaS risk.

## Implementation

(1) Enrich Palo Alto traffic with service/ASN/country; (2) maintain approved_egress.csv; (3) schedule UC every 15m; (4) hit opens a ticket to service owner; (5) 48h SLA; (6) weekly review by Platform team. Indexes required: `index=netfw`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pan:traffic`, `sourcetype=pan:traffic;`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=netfw` sourcetype=pan:traffic; ASN/country enrichment via `asn_country.csv`; approved-egress registry `approved_egress.csv`..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Enrich Palo Alto traffic with service/ASN/country; (2) maintain approved_egress.csv; (3) schedule UC every 15m; (4) hit opens a ticket to service owner; (5) 48h SLA; (6) weekly review by Platform team.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=netfw sourcetype=pan:traffic action=allow direction=outbound earliest=-15m
| rename src AS src_ip dest AS dest_ip
| lookup service_by_src.csv src_ip OUTPUT src_service
| lookup asn_country.csv ip=dest_ip OUTPUT asn AS dest_asn country AS dest_country
| lookup approved_egress.csv src_service dest_asn OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved) OR approved!="yes"
| stats count sum(bytes_out) AS bytes BY src_service dest_ip dest_asn dest_country
```

Understanding this SPL

**SOC 2 CC6.7 — System boundary & data-transmission control: unapproved egress destinations** — Provides the evidence SOC 2 auditors want: proof that outbound flows are governed and deviations are captured. Supports reduction of shadow SaaS risk.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=netfw` sourcetype=pan:traffic; ASN/country enrichment via `asn_country.csv`; approved-egress registry `approved_egress.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: netfw; **sourcetype**: pan:traffic. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=netfw, sourcetype=pan:traffic, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Renames fields with `rename` for clarity or joins.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(approved) OR approved!="yes"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src_service dest_ip dest_asn dest_country** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Geo map of unapproved destinations, bar chart by service, single value 'unapproved egress flows (1h)'.

## SPL

```spl
index=netfw sourcetype=pan:traffic action=allow direction=outbound earliest=-15m
| rename src AS src_ip dest AS dest_ip
| lookup service_by_src.csv src_ip OUTPUT src_service
| lookup asn_country.csv ip=dest_ip OUTPUT asn AS dest_asn country AS dest_country
| lookup approved_egress.csv src_service dest_asn OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved) OR approved!="yes"
| stats count sum(bytes_out) AS bytes BY src_service dest_ip dest_asn dest_country
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src span=1d | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Geo map of unapproved destinations, bar chart by service, single value 'unapproved egress flows (1h)'. Key fields: `bytes`, `dest_asn`, `dest_country`, `dest_ip`, `src_ip`, `src_service`.
